reason: Project spec index (edit/add more spec files).

summary: Conservative closeout landed for continuous-send: POST /messages now accepts and persists user messages immediately, the orchestrator drains one active conversation turn at a time, queued batches preserve pre-dispatch assistant context, failed batches stay pending for retry instead of advancing the consumed boundary, and the chat UI keeps input enabled while stop/delete follow runtime busy state guardrails.

Cross-layer closeout follow-up: fixed the queue-depth aggregation typecheck issue, extended the backend DELETE guard to reject queued conversations from runtime queueDepth as a server-side backstop, and added a smoke/controller regression for direct queued deletion attempts.

Closed the final recovery gap for continuous-send: queue drain failures now surface as runtime-visible queueFailure metadata, the chat UI exposes stuck queued batches and allows an explicit force-delete discard path only when the conversation is idle after a recorded drain failure, and the backend DELETE route accepts that narrow force override while keeping active/dispatching deletions blocked.
